title: Login Screen Layout Restructuring
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the login screen to display a clean, centered login form alongside a 'What's New' section, so that the first experience feels professional and informative.
description:
Restructure the login screen HTML and CSS to achieve a proper two-column layout with centered login form.

## Changes
1. Replace `page-header min-vh-100` class with `content-wrapper`
2. Remove unnecessary wrapper div layer
3. Set column layout to equal 6/6 split instead of 5/7
4. Add inner `dtp-loginbox` div with `max-width: 375px` centered in the left column
5. Apply `background-color: #F4F6F9` to the body

## Layout Structure
- Left column: Centered login box with DTP logo, environment selector (Production/Sandbox), Connect button, license agreement link
- Right column: What's New section with feature highlights (e.g., ERD feature announcement)
acceptance_criteria: Login page uses content-wrapper class instead of page-header
Login form is centered in a max-width 375px container
What's New section displays on the right column
ux_changes: Login screen restructured with a centered dtp-loginbox container, equal column split, and body background color update for a cleaner, more professional appearance.
implementation_notes: Ryan provided the exact HTML structure and dtp-loginbox class. Developer implemented the layout changes and applied the body background color.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the layout changes. Ryan noted a missing body background style that needed to be applied.
